message:
fix(daemon,hook): hash project root, not CWD, for socket path (#3)

A daemon started in /path/A and a hook fired from /path/A/sub
silently miss each other: both hashed the immediate CWD, so the
socket names diverged on any sub-directory invocation. The hook
then fell through to its (broken — see #4) embedded fallback,
producing a rail that never blocked anything.

Fix walks up from CWD looking for the nearest .slb/ ancestor and
hashes that path instead. Identical logic on both sides:

  internal/daemon/client.go::DefaultSocketPath  (Go, daemon side)
  internal/cli/hook.go::_project_root_for_socket (Python template,
                                                  hook side)

So a daemon started anywhere inside `/path/A/...` and a hook
fired anywhere inside `/path/A/...` converge on
sha256(/path/A)[:12]. Two distinct projects still get distinct
sockets — per-project isolation is preserved.

Falls back to raw-CWD hashing if no .slb/ exists in the ancestor
chain, which preserves v0.3.x behavior for installations that
haven't run `slb init`. The fallback is deterministic for a fixed
CWD, so it doesn't introduce non-determinism.

Tests:

- internal/daemon/client_test.go::TestDefaultSocketPath_WalksUp-
  ToProjectRoot creates two project trees, asserts sub-directory
  invocations within project A converge on the same socket, and
  that project A and project B produce different sockets.
- internal/daemon/client_test.go::TestDefaultSocketPath_NoProject-
  RootFallsBackToCWD asserts the fallback is deterministic.
- End-to-end smoke test against a freshly-generated slb_guard.py
  confirms the same convergence/divergence shape on the Python
  side.

Note: option (4) from the issue body (stderr warning when the
daemon socket isn't found) is not included here — the silent
fallback is intentional in slb's existing design and adding a
warning would create noise on every hook fire that's the daemon
is just not running. The architectural fix (#3 itself) makes the
warning unnecessary for the multi-CWD case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -496,10 +496,35 @@ import tempfile
 
 SLB_TIMEOUT = 0.05  # 50ms timeout
 
+def _project_root_for_socket(start: str) -> str:
+    """Walk up from start looking for a .slb/ directory and return
+    its parent. Falls back to start if no .slb/ ancestor exists.
+
+    Mirrors the Go-side projectRootForSocket helper so the hook and
+    daemon converge on the same socket name even when fired from
+    different sub-directories of the same project (issue #3).
+    """
+    try:
+        path = os.path.abspath(start)
+    except Exception:
+        return start
+    while True:
+        candidate = os.path.join(path, ".slb")
+        if os.path.isdir(candidate):
+            return path
+        parent = os.path.dirname(path)
+        if parent == path:
+            # Hit filesystem root without finding .slb/ — fall back
+            # to the original CWD to preserve v0.3.x behavior on
+            # installations that have not run "slb init".
+            return os.path.abspath(start)
+        path = parent
+
 def get_socket_path() -> str:
-    """Get the SLB daemon socket path for the current directory."""
+    """Get the SLB daemon socket path for the current project."""
     cwd = os.getcwd()
-    hash_digest = hashlib.sha256(cwd.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
+    hash_base = _project_root_for_socket(cwd)
+    hash_digest = hashlib.sha256(hash_base.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
     return os.path.join(tempfile.gettempdir(), f"slb-{hash_digest}.sock")
 
 def query_slb_daemon(command: str, session_id: str, cwd: str) -> Optional[dict]:
